body_txt: A person is replaced at some point in the plot by an imposter, but their original friends or the people that work with them prefer the imposter. It turns out that the original, while being the real one, is a lousy person and/or not good at what they do. The fake, on the other hand, turns out to be better in general. They are kind to puppies, they actually love the wife instead of treating her as a sex object, and they are a good person in general. The imposter might be a better leader/manager. When the original comes back, nobody wants them around. Expect this if employed by a villain to possibly be a Heel–Face Turn moment, as they realize virtues they didn't know they had pretending to be someone else, or learn that Good Feels Good . See also Becoming the Mask . See also Effective Knockoff for objects and devices and Prefers the Illusion for entire realities, rather than characters. Compare Redeeming Replacement and Suspiciously Similar Substitute . Contrast We Want Our Jerk Back! . Can be considered an In-Universe version of More Popular Replacement . "Better If Not Born" Plot is another way to make a character find out no one likes him.
